active recall.definition: The cognitive practice of pulling information from memory instead of passively reviewing notes, strengthening neural synapses.
The psychological phenomenon where taking practice quizzes produces superior long-term retention compared to mere re-reading.

spaced repetition.definition: Spacing learning intervals over time using cognitive decay models to optimize review schedules just before the forgetting curve triggers.
An algorithmic approach computing specific interval multipliers based on feedback scale indices of recall ease.

feynman technique.definition: Explaining concepts in extremely basic terms to identify gaps in personal understanding, mapping knowledge barriers.

leitner system.definition: A simple manual method of spaced repetition where flashcards are moved into further boxes on success, or reset to box one on failure.
